title: Common Dividend Declared by NNN REIT, Inc.
author: Prnewswire
description: NNN REIT, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $0.60 per share payable May 15, 2026 to shareholders of record as of April 30, 2026. The company is one of only three publicly traded REITs to have increased its annual dividend for 36 or more consecutive years. As of December 31, 2025, NNN owned 3,692 properties across all 50 states with approximately 39.6 million square feet of gross leasable area and a weighted average remaining lease term of 10.2 years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a consistent quarterly dividend and maintains an impressive 36+ year streak of consecutive annual dividend increases, demonstrating financial stability and shareholder-friendly capital allocation. The substantial property portfolio with long-term leases provides stable cash flows.

title: Realty Income is Great, But NNN Does One Thing Better
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: While Realty Income is a well-run net lease REIT with a 31-year dividend increase streak, NNN REIT has an even more impressive 36-year streak. Despite being smaller with a $7.9B market cap versus Realty Income's $56B, NNN REIT's smaller size allows it to be more selective with acquisitions and grow its dividend more easily, offering a higher 5.7% yield compared to Realty Income's 5.3%.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Recognized as a very well-run company with an impressive 31-year streak of annual dividend increases and strong market position as the largest net lease REIT with $56B market cap and global diversification.

Highlighted as having a superior 36-year dividend increase streak, higher dividend yield (5.7% vs 5.3%), and strategic advantages from its smaller size including selective acquisition practices and stronger relationship-driven growth opportunities.

title: Common Dividend Declared by NNN REIT, Inc.
author: Prnewswire
description: NNN REIT, Inc. announced a quarterly dividend of $0.60 per share payable on February 13, 2026 to shareholders of record as of January 30, 2026. The company is one of only three publicly traded REITs to have increased its annual dividend for 36 or more consecutive years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company declared a dividend payment and maintains an impressive 36+ year streak of consecutive annual dividend increases, demonstrating financial stability and commitment to shareholder returns. This is a notable achievement among publicly traded REITs.

title: NNN REIT, Inc. Announces 2025 Dividend Tax Status
author: Prnewswire
description: NNN REIT announced that 95.3188% of its 2025 dividends paid to common shareholders are classified as taxable distributions for federal income tax purposes. The company paid $2.36 per share in common dividends in 2025, marking its 36th consecutive annual increase. As of September 30, 2025, NNN owned 3,697 properties across 50 states with a weighted average remaining lease term of 10.1 years.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company achieved its 36th consecutive annual dividend increase, demonstrating consistent growth and financial strength. The CFO highlighted disciplined investment strategy, prudent balance sheet management, and a durable, diversified portfolio with a strong 10.1-year weighted average lease term, indicating operational stability and shareholder value creation.

title: 3 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Set to Shine After the Fed’s Next Rate Cut
author: Timothy Fries
description: The article discusses three dividend stocks poised to perform well after the anticipated Federal Reserve rate cut, highlighting NNN REIT, Verizon, and Amcor as potential investment opportunities in a soft landing economic scenario.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Dividend aristocrat with 36 consecutive years of dividend increases, strong property portfolio, increasing annualized base rent, and positive price target
Undergoing significant workforce reduction and restructuring, with potential long-term efficiency gains but short-term challenges from layoffs and severance costs
Strong growth in flexible and rigid packaging divisions, reaffirmed fiscal outlook, bullish analyst ratings, and benefiting from e-commerce and food delivery packaging trends
